_comment: Chrome recommended for Operaton (Firefox has CSP reload issues)

camunda7.readme:
The original open-source BPMN engine that started it all in 2013. Camunda 7 is battle-tested, rock-solid, and trusted by thousands of organizations worldwide — from startups to Fortune 500 companies. Over 400 enterprise customers and millions of community downloads prove its reliability.

Built on Spring Boot and runs on the JVM (Java 8+). Supports BPMN 2.0, CMMN 1.1, and DMN 1.3 standards with full specification compliance. Features embedded process engine that runs in-process with your Java application, or as a standalone server. Includes powerful REST API, external task pattern for polyglot microservices, and comprehensive web applications (Cockpit for monitoring, Tasklist for human workflows, Admin for system management).

Key Strengths:
• Mature ecosystem with 10+ years of production use
• Extensive connector library (500+ community extensions)
• Rich monitoring and debugging tools (Cockpit dashboards)
• Java-native with deep Spring integration
• Self-hosted deployment gives you full control
• Active community forums and Stack Overflow presence

Best For: Traditional enterprise Java shops, regulated industries requiring on-premise deployment, teams wanting full control over their process engine, organizations with existing Camunda 7 expertise.

Official Resources:
• Website: https://camunda.com/platform-7/
• Documentation: https://docs.camunda.org/manual/latest/
• Community Forum: https://forum.camunda.io/
• GitHub: https://github.com/camunda/camunda-bpm-platform

Note: Camunda officially ended support for the Community Edition in October 2024. For continued open-source support, consider Operaton (the community fork) or evaluate Camunda 8 for cloud-native needs.

operaton.readme:
The community-driven fork keeping Camunda 7 CE alive after its official end-of-life in October 2024. Born from the open-source community's commitment to accessibility and freedom, Operaton ensures BPMN remains open and free for everyone.

100% API-compatible with Camunda 7.24 — same Java API, same REST endpoints, same database schema, same BPMN XML format. Drop-in replacement that runs your existing processes without modification. Migrate from Camunda 7 CE in minutes by swapping JAR files. Supports all standard connectors, element templates, and integrations. Continues the proven Spring Boot architecture with full BPMN 2.0, CMMN 1.1, and DMN 1.3 support.

Key Strengths:
• True open-source under Apache 2.0 license (no enterprise upsell)
• Zero lock-in — migrate back to Camunda 7 anytime
• Active development roadmap with monthly releases
• Community-first governance (transparent decision-making)
• Modernization focus (Java 17+, cloud-native features)
• All Camunda 7 connectors and extensions work unchanged

Best For: Organizations wanting to avoid vendor lock-in, teams committed to open-source principles, existing Camunda 7 CE users looking for continued support, startups and SMEs needing enterprise-grade BPMN without licensing costs.

Operaton vs Camunda 7:
• Operaton: Community-governed, always open-source, no commercial licensing
• Camunda 7: Enterprise support available, larger corporate backing, eventual EOL path

Official Resources:
• Website: https://operaton.org/
• Documentation: https://docs.operaton.org/
• GitHub: https://github.com/operaton/operaton
• Community Chat: https://discord.gg/operaton

Fun Fact: "Operaton" comes from "Operation" + "Automaton" — symbolizing automated business operations powered by community collaboration.

cib-seven.readme:
Enterprise-grade BPMN platform built on Camunda 7's proven foundation, enhanced for regulated industries and mission-critical workloads. Developed by CIB (Cologne Intelligence GmbH), a German software company with 25+ years of experience in banking and insurance sectors.

Enhanced version of Camunda 7 maintaining full API compatibility while adding enterprise capabilities: advanced monitoring dashboards, performance optimization for high-throughput scenarios (10k+ instances/sec), high-availability clustering with automatic failover, and enterprise support SLA with 24/7 coverage. Includes additional security features (role-based access control, audit logging, encryption at rest), compliance certifications (ISO 27001, SOC 2, GDPR-ready), and regulatory reporting tools.

Key Strengths:
• Certified for financial services and healthcare compliance
• German engineering and data sovereignty guarantees
• Performance-optimized for complex process models
• Advanced operations tooling and APM integration
• Professional services and migration support included
• Long-term support (LTS) releases with 5-year lifecycle

Best For: Banks, insurance companies, healthcare providers, government agencies, large enterprises in regulated industries, organizations requiring compliance certifications and professional support SLAs.

CIB Seven vs Camunda 7:
• CIB Seven: Enterprise features, compliance certifications, professional support
• Camunda 7: Community/self-service, standard features, community support

Official Resources:
• Website: https://www.cib.de/en/cib-seven/
• Documentation: https://docs.cib.de/seven/
• Contact: enterprise@cib.de

Coming Soon: CIB Seven integration is planned for a future Catalyst Studio release. Contact us if you'd like early access or have specific requirements.

camunda8.readme:
Cloud-native workflow orchestration powered by Zeebe, Camunda's next-generation process engine. Built from the ground up for horizontal scalability, microservices architecture, and modern cloud deployments. Fundamentally different from Camunda 7 — not a migration path, but a greenfield platform.

Event-driven workflow engine using Raft consensus protocol for distributed, fault-tolerant deployment. Designed for Kubernetes with horizontal auto-scaling, multi-tenancy, and cloud-native observability (Prometheus, Grafana). gRPC-based client libraries for Java, Node.js, Go, C#, Python. Optimized for extreme throughput (100k+ process instances per second). Stateless workers enable polyglot task execution across any language or platform.

Key Strengths:
• Horizontally scalable with no single point of failure
• Cloud-native architecture (SaaS or self-managed)
• Language-agnostic worker model (not Java-only)
• Built-in resilience and automatic recovery
• Modern developer experience (GraphQL API, Web Modeler)
• Enterprise features included in SaaS tier

Best For: Cloud-native applications, microservices architectures, high-throughput scenarios, organizations standardizing on Kubernetes, teams wanting managed SaaS option, greenfield projects with no Camunda 7 legacy.

Camunda 8 vs Camunda 7:
• Architecture: Event-driven (C8) vs embedded/standalone (C7)
• Deployment: Cloud-first (C8) vs on-premise focus (C7)
• Language support: Polyglot (C8) vs Java-centric (C7)
• Throughput: 100k+ instances/sec (C8) vs 10k instances/sec (C7)
• Migration: NOT compatible — requires process redesign

Official Resources:
• Website: https://camunda.com/platform/
• Documentation: https://docs.camunda.io/
• SaaS Trial: https://signup.camunda.com/
• GitHub: https://github.com/camunda/zeebe
• Community Forum: https://forum.camunda.io/

Coming Soon: Camunda 8 integration is planned for a future Catalyst Studio release. The architecture differences mean it will be a separate deployment option, not a drop-in replacement for Camunda 7/Operaton.

eximee.readme:
Low-code BPMN platform with advanced form builder, document management, and case management capabilities. Slovak-based platform popular in Central and Eastern European government and healthcare sectors. Combines process automation with visual development tools for rapid application delivery.

Built on Spring Boot with embedded Camunda 7 engine core (API-compatible), enhanced with proprietary extensions: visual form designer with 50+ field types, integrated document management system (DMS) with OCR and e-signature support, low-code integration builder (REST, SOAP, database connectors), and dynamic UI generation. Features drag-and-drop process modeling, conditional form logic, workflow-driven DMS with version control, and automatic RESTful API generation. Supports BPMN 2.0 with extended custom task types for forms and documents.

Key Strengths:
• Complete low-code platform (forms + workflows + documents)
• Rapid application development (weeks instead of months)
• Pre-built templates for government processes
• Multilingual UI (20+ languages including Slovak, Czech, Polish)
• On-premise deployment with data sovereignty
• Professional services for implementation and training

Best For: Government agencies, healthcare providers, insurance companies in CEE region, organizations needing forms + workflows + documents in one platform, teams wanting low-code development with BPMN standard compliance.

eximeeBPMS vs Camunda 7:
• eximeeBPMS: Low-code platform with forms/DMS built-in
• Camunda 7: Developer-focused with external form/DMS integration

Official Resources:
• Website: https://www.eximee.eu/
• Documentation: https://docs.eximee.eu/
• Demo: https://demo.eximee.eu/
• Contact: info@eximee.eu

Coming Soon: eximeeBPMS integration is planned for a future Catalyst Studio release. The forms and DMS capabilities make it ideal for rapid prototyping and citizen-facing government applications.
